title_en: One Vigilante, 22 Cell Tower Fires, and a World of Conspiracies
title_zh: 一个“义警”、22起基站失火，以及一场阴谋论风暴

paragraphs.en: As dawn spread over San Antonio on September 9, 2021, almond-colored smoke began to fill the sky above the city’s Far West Side. The plumes were whorling off the top of a 132-foot-tall cell tower that overshadows an office park just north of SeaWorld. At a hotel a mile away, a paramedic snapped a photo of the spectacle and posted it to the r/sanantonio subreddit. “Cell tower on fire around 1604 and Culebra,” he wrote.
paragraphs.zh: 2021年9月9日，圣安东尼奥迎来拂晓时，杏仁色的浓烟开始充满这座城市西侧边缘上空。烟柱从一座132英尺高的通讯基站顶部盘旋升起，这座基站坐落在紧挨着SeaWorld以北的一处办公园区上空投下阴影。离那里一英里外的一家酒店里，一名急救医师拍下了这幕，并把照片发到了r/sanantonio板块。他写道：“Cell tower on fire around 1604 and Culebra。”

paragraphs.en: In typical Reddit fashion, the comments section piled up with corny jokes. “Blazing 5G speeds,” quipped one user.
paragraphs.zh: 像Reddit一向那样，评论区很快被低俗玩笑刷满。“5G高速飞起（Blazing 5G speeds）。”一位用户打趣道。

paragraphs.en: “I hope no one inhales those fumes, the Covid transmission via 5G will be a lot more potent that way,” wrote another, in a swipe at the conspiracy theorists who claim that radiation from 5G towers caused the Covid-19 pandemic.
paragraphs.zh: 另一位则写道：“希望没有人吸入这些烟雾，不然通过5G传播新冠的毒性会更强。”这句话直指那些宣称5G基站辐射导致新冠肺炎疫情的阴谋论者。

paragraphs.en: The wisecracks went on: “Can you hear me now?”
paragraphs.zh: 玩笑还在继续：“你现在听得到我吗？”

paragraphs.en: “Great, some hero trying to save us from 5G.”
paragraphs.zh: “太好了，又有个英雄想把我们从5G中解救出来。”

paragraphs.en: That self-styled hero was actually lurking in the comments. As he followed the thread on his phone, Sean Aaron Smith delighted in the sheer volume of attention the tower fire was receiving, even if most of it dripped with sarcasm. A lean, tattooed—and until recently, entirely apolitical—27-year-old, Smith had come to view 5G as the linchpin of a globalist plot to zombify humanity. To resist that supposed scheme, he’d spent the past five months setting Texas cell towers ablaze.
paragraphs.zh: 这个自称英雄的人其实就在评论区里“潜伏”着。趁着他一边在手机上追着这个话题线索往下看，肖恩·艾伦·史密斯（Sean Aaron Smith）乐在其中，沉浸在那些围绕基站起火事宜的大量关注中，即便其中大多数都夹杂着讽刺。这个身材瘦削、身上有纹身的27岁年轻人——直到最近还算完全不涉政治——逐渐把5G视为一场让人类“僵尸化”的全球主义阴谋的核心。为了抵制他所认为的这项阴谋，他过去五个月一直在德州点燃和焚烧蜂窝基站。

paragraphs.en: Smith’s crude and quixotic campaign against 5G was precisely the sort of security threat that was fast becoming one of the US government’s top concerns in 2021. Just two weeks after Smith’s fire popped up on Reddit, then FBI director Christopher Wray discussed the latest trends in political violence in a speech marking the 20th anniversary of the 9/11 attacks. “Today, the greatest terrorist threat we face here in the US is from what are, in effect, lone actors,” he said, describing these people as moving “quickly from radicalization to action, often using easily obtainable weapons against soft targets.” And an increasing number of these individuals, Wray stressed, were turning violent after marinating in bizarre conspiracy theories.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯那场粗糙而近乎愚勇的反5G运动，正好是2021年美国政府迅速把它列为最严峻安全威胁之一的那类威胁。就在史密斯的这场“纵火”出现在Reddit上后的短短两周后，时任联邦调查局局长克里斯托弗·雷伊（Christopher Wray）在纪念“9·11”袭击20周年的讲话中，谈到了近期政治暴力的趋势。他说：“在美国，我们面临的最大恐怖主义威胁，实际上来自所谓的孤立行动者。”他把这些人描述为“很快就会从极端化走向行动”，并且“经常使用容易获得的武器袭击软目标”。雷伊强调，越来越多的人在长期沉迷离奇阴谋论之后，最终走向了暴力。

paragraphs.en: In the years since Wray first delivered that warning, political violence in the US has continued to evolve much as he foresaw. Numerous recent attacks have been launched by people whose media diets have conditioned them to believe that government oppressors, permissive liberals, or shadowy cabals must be stopped at all costs. “This conspiracy stuff, it’s not coming from HitlerLover4Chan88 on Twitter anymore,” says Jonathan Lewis, a research fellow at George Washington University’s Program on Extremism. “It’s coming from a blue check, a gold check, a verified account—someone who, for a lot of people, has legitimacy.” He adds that some of those paranoid influencers are even operating in the halls of power. “You’ve got Groypers running Department of Homeland Security Twitter accounts,” Lewis says. “You’re getting legislative bills being passed about climate modification.”
paragraphs.zh: 自雷伊首次发出警告以来，美国的政治暴力一直在按他预见的方向持续演变。最近发生的许多袭击，都由一些人发动——这些人的媒介输入已经把他们塑造成相信政府压迫者、宽松派自由主义者，或者黑暗中的秘密集团，必须以任何代价被制止。乔纳森·刘易斯（Jonathan Lewis）——乔治华盛顿大学极端主义项目的一名研究员——说：“这类阴谋内容，现在不再主要来自Twitter上的‘HitlerLover4Chan88’那样的账号。”他接着说：“它来自蓝勾、金勾、经过认证的账号——也就是说，在很多人看来，这样的人是有‘可信度’的。”刘易斯补充说，一些偏执的影响者甚至已经在权力核心里“作业”了。“你甚至会看到，负责国土安全部推特账号的人里有Groypers。”刘易斯说，“你会看到关于气候改造的立法法案正在被通过。”

paragraphs.en: Once convinced that violence is the only moral choice, lone actors are routinely carrying out hit-and-run attacks against pieces of the nation’s technological infrastructure, which remain lightly guarded despite their vast importance. The types of sites being targeted are as varied as the causes that motivate their attackers. In 2022, for example, someone shot up two electrical substations in North Carolina, in a possible far-right effort to disrupt a drag show. Two years later, a Tennessee man was arrested for allegedly plotting to use drones to bomb Nashville’s power grid in hopes of hastening a race war. This past July, a member of a militia group that trafficked in weather-manipulation conspiracy theories allegedly smashed up an Oklahoma radar station. And saboteurs with unknown motives have also been severing fiber-optic cables in both California and Missouri since the early summer. (Gauging the true number of infrastructure attacks has become more difficult since the DHS shuttered its Terrorism and Targeted Violence database in March.)
paragraphs.zh: 一旦人们深信暴力是唯一道德选择，单独行动者往往会对国家的重要技术基础设施发动“撞车式”袭击，这些设施虽举足轻重却防护薄弱。被瞄准的目标类型与驱动袭击者的动机一样复杂。比如，2022年，某人向北卡罗来纳州的两个变电站开火，可能是出于极右翼意图想要中断一场变装秀。两年后，一名田纳西州男子被捕，涉嫌计划用无人机轰炸纳什维尔电网，以期加速一场种族战争。就在今年7月，一名参与传播“天气操控”阴谋论的民兵组织成员据称砸坏了俄克拉荷马州的一处雷达站。另有动机不明的破坏者，自今年早夏以来还曾在加利福尼亚和密苏里州切断光纤电缆。（自3月国土安全部关闭“恐怖主义与有针对性暴力”数据库后，准确统计基础设施攻击的真实数量变得更加困难。）

paragraphs.en: But Smith—who planned and executed his arsons by himself—appears to have been more prolific than any of these other extremists. The blaze north of SeaWorld was the seventh he’d set in 2021; in the seven months that followed, he would burn another 15. I spent the past year talking to Smith at length about the origin and details of his anti-5G crusade. I did so in the hope of learning how and why some desperate souls are being lured into destroying the guts of modern life.
paragraphs.zh: 但斯密斯——他独自策划并实施了纵火——似乎比其他任何极端分子都更为频繁。去年他在海洋世界北侧点起的火是他在2021年放火的第七起；随后七个月里，他又放了15起。过去一年里，我与斯密斯有过长时间交谈，了解他那场反5G运动的起源和细节。我这样做，是想弄清楚是什么原因将一些绝望的人引向拆解现代生活“内脏”的行为。

paragraphs.en: Smith grew up in a three-bedroom trailer in northwest San Antonio, the only child of a carpenter and a nurse’s assistant. He entered high school with dreams of making a career in the military, but his life swerved when his father slipped into alcoholism after a construction accident. With his dad lost in the haze of drink and his mother rarely home due to her 12-hour work shifts, Smith searched elsewhere for belonging. He dropped out of high school in the 10th grade to devote himself to a new group of friends whose sole interests were drugs and petty crime. “The attention they gave me inspired a fierce loyalty,” he says. “There’s not much I wouldn’t do for them.” At the age of 18, he went to prison for burglarizing a house while blacked out on Xanax. He would spend the bulk of the next six years behind bars after blowing parole and picking up multiple drug and firearm charges; his father died of cancer while he was locked up.
paragraphs.zh: 斯密斯在圣安东尼奥西北部的一辆三居室拖车里长大，是一名木匠和一名护士助理的独生子。上高中的时候，他曾梦想走军旅生涯，但父亲在一次建筑事故后酗酒后，他的人生便偏离了轨道。父亲陷入酒精迷雾而母亲又常因12小时轮班而难得在家，斯密斯于是到别处寻找归属感。他在高二辍学，投入一群以毒品和轻微犯罪为主要兴趣的新朋友中。“他们给我的关注让我生出了强烈的忠诚感，”他说，“为了他们，我几乎什么都能做。”18岁时，他在服用氙卓唑仑失去意识后入室盗窃，被判入狱。此后六年里他大部分时间都在监狱中度过，原因是违反假释并再次涉入多起毒品和枪支指控；他父亲在他被关押期间死于癌症。

paragraphs.en: When Smith was paroled from his third prison stint in November 2019, he resolved to make a break with his troubled past. He moved back into his mother’s trailer and landed a cooking job at Golden Corral. He also started a relationship with 18-year-old Coley Lane Dupre, a former elite gymnast who’d recently turned rebellious; she had moved next door to Smith after walking out of drug rehab. All was going smoothly until March 2020, when the pandemic shuttered Smith’s restaurant and the gymnastics studio where Dupre coached. Their new lives were forced into suspended animation, leaving the couple to fill the days with getting high and scrolling.
paragraphs.zh: 2019年11月，斯密斯第三次入狱后获释时，决定与自己 troubled 的过去彻底切断。他搬回母亲的拖车居住，并在 Golden Corral 找到一份厨师工作。他也开始与18岁的柯莉·莱恩·迪普尔（Coley Lane Dupre）交往，她曾是精英体操运动员，最近却变得叛逆；她从戒毒所离开后搬到了斯密斯隔壁。直到2020年3月，疫情导致斯密斯的餐馆和迪普尔任教的体操馆都关闭，一切进展都很顺利的生活骤然陷入停滞，迫使这对情侣只能用“嗑药”和刷屏来消磨日子。

paragraphs.en: During one of those bleary sessions, a videoclip from episode 1,308 of The Joe Rogan Experience, which had originally aired in June 2019, popped up in Smith’s Instagram feed. The clip begins with Rogan pausing to light a joint, which gives his guest—Eddie Bravo, an anvil-headed jiujitsu master known for spouting conspiracies about 9/11 and a flat Earth—the opportunity to pose a question: “What do you think of, um, 5G and all that scare? You think that’s legit?” Rogan pronounces himself “terrified” of the wireless networking technology, which was then starting to become widespread in the US. “How much long-term testing have they done?” he asks, without specifying the potential effects he fears. “Zero?” Bravo then urges Rogan to visit a website that contains the government’s “400-page plan for the world,” a document that he says contains revelations about 5G that will “get your fucking head blown off.”
paragraphs.zh: 在一次昏昏沉沉的熬夜时分，史密斯的 Instagram 动态里跳出一段《The Joe Rogan Experience》第1308期的短视频。这段视频最初播出于2019年6月。片头是罗根停下动作去点燃一支大麻烟卷，这给了嘉宾——埃迪·布拉沃——发问的机会。布拉沃是一位以谈论911阴谋和“扁平地球”著称的“砧头”柔术大师。他问道：“你觉得5G什么的、那些恐惧情绪，真的假的？你觉得它有道理吗？”罗根称自己对无线网络技术“非常害怕”，而当时该技术在美国才刚开始大规模普及。“他们做了多少长期测试？”他问道，却没有说明自己担心的具体后果。“零次？”布拉沃于是劝罗根去一个网站看看，那里有政府的“400页世界计划”——他称其中揭露了关于5G的内容，会把你“操你妈的脑袋都给吹飞了”。

paragraphs.en: The clip was short on details, but it reminded Smith of a man he’d befriended in prison in 2014. This inmate had been an avid reader of books about magnets and electricity, and he’d often rambled about a sprawling communications “grid” that he said was being built for nefarious reasons. Smith had never paid much attention to these convoluted rants, but the two famous podcasters seemed to be echoing his friend’s concerns.
paragraphs.zh: 这段片段内容不多，但让史密斯想起了他2014年在监狱里结识的一位男囚。那位犯人热衷于阅读有关磁场和电力的书，常常滔滔不绝地谈论一个“正在被建立、出于邪恶目的”的庞大通信“电网”。史密斯过去并未认真关注过这些盘根错节、绕来绕去的言论，但现在，他看到这两位著名播客人似乎在重复自己那位朋友的担忧。

paragraphs.en: When he plugged queries about 5G into search bars, Smith quickly became alarmed. Social media was awash in posts asserting that 5G towers, which use higher electromagnetic frequencies than their 4G forerunners, had weakened human immune systems, leaving them unable to fend off the Covid-19 virus. (Several nations where 5G was not yet available in 2020, such as Iran, were among the most devastated by Covid.) Prominent figures like the actor Woody Harrelson claimed on Insta-gram (falsely) that China was dismantling its 5G towers to curb Covid’s spread. As Smith’s social-media algorithms adjusted to his affinity for these posts, he became aware that arsonists in the United Kingdom had set fire to more than 60 cell towers in the spring of 2020. This spate of violence had been inspired in part by the YouTube sermons of a British pastor and crypto consultant who preached that “the radio frequencies we are being exposed to are killing the people.” (The pastor gained legitimacy by claiming to be a former executive at Vodafone; he had in fact been a salesman for the telecommunications company for less than a year, well before the rollout of 5G.)
paragraphs.zh: 当他在搜索框里输入5G相关问题时，史密斯很快被警醒。社交媒体上到处都是帖子，声称5G基站使用比4G更高的电磁频率，已经削弱了人体免疫系统，使人无法抵挡新冠病毒。（2020年，那些当时还没有部署5G的国家，如伊朗，反而是受新冠重创最严重的国家之一。）像演员伍迪·哈里森这样知名人士在Instagram上（错误地）宣称，中国正在拆除其5G基站以遏制新冠传播。随着算法发现他对这类内容的偏好，史密斯还发现，英国有纵火者在2020年春季焚毁了60多座基站。这股暴力浪潮部分受一位英国牧师兼加密货币顾问的YouTube“布道”影响，他宣称“我们暴露在的无线频率正在杀人”。（这位牧师曾借口自己是沃达丰前高管来增加可信度，实际上他只是这家电信公司销售人员，任期不足一年，而且还是在5G推出之前。）

paragraphs.en: Smith’s suspicions only deepened when he noticed that his favorite 5G content often vanished within hours of being posted. “If you said anything connecting 5G with Covid, you were censored, Facebook is taking your post down, it was fact-checking you,” he says. “And I’m like, why are they so worried about censoring people on the subject? And that’s kind of what resonated with me.” Smith concluded that powerful forces were concealing the insidious truth about 5G.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯的怀疑只会越发加深，因为他注意到自己最喜欢的5G内容常在发布后几个小时内就消失了。“如果你说了任何把5G和新冠联系在一起的话，你就会被审查，Facebook会把你的帖子拿下，还会对你做事实核查。”他回忆道。“而我就在想，他们为什么对这个话题上的言论审查如此上心？这正是触动我的地方。”史密斯得出结论：有更强大的势力在掩盖有关5G的隐蔽真相。

paragraphs.en: With no shot at landing another restaurant gig in the midst of the pandemic, Smith backslid into selling drugs as the spring of 2020 wore on. He roamed San Antonio’s eerily deserted streets, delivering cannabis, cocaine, and other intoxicants to customers hunkered down in their homes. During his rounds, he noticed that 5G towers were some of the only structures still being built. He occasionally pulled over to ask construction workers why they were risking illness and death to expand the rollout of 5G. When they told him to scram, he considered their rudeness yet another red flag.
paragraphs.zh: 在疫情期间，史密斯已经没有机会再接到餐厅的工作，于是随着2020年春天的到来，他又回到了贩毒行列。圣安东尼奥的街道一片诡异的寂静，他穿梭其中，把大麻、可卡因和其他致幻品送到躲在家中的顾客那里。在巡回送货时，他注意到，5G 塔是仅有仍在建设中的少数建筑之一。他有时会停下车来问工人们，为什么要冒着生病甚至死亡的风险去推进 5G 的铺设。工人们让他“滚蛋”时，他把这种粗鲁当成另一个警讯。

paragraphs.en: When Smith wasn’t out dealing, he was usually alone in the trailer with Dupre—his mother was working even crazier hours than usual at a hospital flooded with Covid patients. The couple would watch videos from InfoWars impresario Alex Jones and the British conspiracy theorist David Icke, the latter of whom is notorious for claiming the world is secretly controlled by reptilian humanoids. These sources were now espousing increasingly dark and elaborate stories about 5G that portrayed the technology as central to a scheme to enslave entire nations. One popular narrative held that governments had unleashed the Covid-19 virus to force people into isolation, thereby giving construction crews the time and space to build out 5G networks. When a vaccine was eventually developed, the radiation from 5G towers would interact with graphene oxide nanomaterials that were integrated into the injections. This would give governments the power to control how their citizens behave or even to annihilate them en masse if they ever revolted. “If 5G continues and reaches where they want to take it,” Icke warned in an April 2020 interview, “human life as we know it is over.”
paragraphs.zh: 当史密斯不在外面跑单时，他通常一个人和杜普雷待在房车里——他的母亲在挤满新冠患者的医院里轮班，工作时长比平时更疯。两人会一起看 InfoWars 的主办人亚历克斯·琼斯和英国阴谋论者大卫·艾克的视频，后者以宣称世界被蜥蜴人类秘密控制而闻名。这些内容如今对 5G 的叙事愈发阴暗、复杂，描绘技术是奴役整国的核心。一个流行说法认为，政府故意放出新冠病毒，把人们逼入隔离，从而给施工队提供了时间和空间来建设 5G 网络。当疫苗最终研发出来后，5G 塔发出的辐射会与注射剂中加入的氧化石墨烯纳米材料发生反应。这将让政府能够控制公民行为，甚至在全民起义时大规模灭绝他们。艾克在2020年4月的一次采访中警告说：“如果5G继续发展并到达他们想要的地方，那么我们所熟知的人类生活就结束了。”

paragraphs.en: This story, often peddled by people who sold garments or knickknacks that purported to repel 5G radiation, made perfect sense to Smith. He’d formerly never had a shred of interest in politics—“My mindset was always, oh, it has nothing to do with me,” he says—but the technophobic content he was now consuming had melted his apathy. “The way that China is right now with the technology and the surveillance, I saw America turning into the same thing eventually,” he says. “Our freedoms deleted, stuff like that.” He made Dupre similarly fearful by showing her several patents that employ the phrase “voice to skull,” which the couple interpreted as evidence that the government could use 5G to implant thoughts in an unsuspecting populace. (The patents in question do not make this claim.)
paragraphs.zh: 这个故事常常由一些售卖所谓能防御 5G 辐射的衣物或小饰品的人大肆传播，对史密斯来说似乎再合理不过。他曾经几乎对政治没有任何兴趣——“我的想法一直是，哦，这和我没关系，”他说——但他如今不断摄入的技术恐惧内容已经融化了他的冷漠。“就看美国现在在技术和监控方面的做法，我看到美国最终会变成和中国一样的模样，”他说。“我们的自由被删掉了，诸如此类。”他还向杜普雷展示了几项使用“voice to skull”短语的专利，试图让她也产生恐惧。两人都把这视为政府可借助 5G 向毫无戒心的人群植入想法的证据。（有争议的那些专利并没有作出这样的主张。）

paragraphs.en: On the evening of Friday, July 17, Smith was en route to a customer’s house when the police pulled him over for a routine traffic stop. He was arrested for drug and firearm possession, two charges that seemed certain to result in the revocation of his parole and a return to prison. But Smith realized that he probably wouldn’t be cited for a parole violation until court reopened on Monday morning. So Dupre scraped together enough money to bail him out on Saturday, and the couple went on the run.
paragraphs.zh: 2020年7月17日星期五傍晚，史密斯正驾车去一位客户家中时，被警察因例行交通拦查而拦下。他因持有毒品和枪支被捕，这两项指控似乎几乎注定会导致他的缓刑被取消并被送回监狱。但史密斯意识到，直到周一上午法庭恢复开庭，他很可能还不会被正式以违反缓刑名义起诉。于是杜普雷在周六凑足了足够的保释金，随后这对伴侣便逃离了。

paragraphs.en: The grim realities of life on the lam complicated Smith and Dupre’s efforts to further their study of 5G. They mostly crashed in local drug houses, where Smith’s profession always made him a welcome presence at first. But something would inevitably go awry after a few days or weeks of rooming with mercurial meth abusers. Both were assaulted and robbed at gunpoint, and Smith and Dupre had to flee one toxic situation after another.
paragraphs.zh: 在流亡生活的严酷现实中，史密斯和杜普雷进一步研究 5G 的努力变得异常复杂。两人多半住进当地的毒品屋，而史密斯的职业身份起初总会让他一开始受到欢迎。但与情绪变化无常的冰毒吸食者一起住上一阵子后，情况总会不可避免地失控。他们都曾在有枪威胁下遭到殴打和抢劫，史密斯和杜普雷不得不一次次从有毒环境中逃离。

paragraphs.en: As the stress of being a fugitive chipped away at Smith’s psyche, he pondered how to recapture a sense of purpose. “I wanted to be a better person,” he says. “I’m still doing these things, I’m still in this lifestyle. But part of me was wanting to help people.” In late November, he decided to take a new step: Instead of passively absorbing information pushed to him by podcasters and algorithms, he would investigate a 5G tower in the wild.
paragraphs.zh: 随着“在逃者”身份带来的压力不断侵蚀史密斯的心理，他开始思考如何重新找回一种人生目标感。“我想成为一个更好的人，”他说。“我现在还在做这些事，我还在这种生活方式里。但我内心有一部分想去帮助别人。”11 月下旬，他决定迈出一步：他不再被动吸收播客和算法不断推送给他的信息，而是要亲自去“野外”调查一座 5G 塔。

paragraphs.en: Smith and Dupre spent a night combing San Antonio for a tower to explore. Most of the structures were inaccessible, protected by chain-link fences topped with razor wire. But the couple finally found an unsecured tower tucked away in a suburban neighborhood. As they approached the site, they were frightened by its loud and eerie hum. (The noise from 5G towers is generated by cooling fans and electrical components.) They believed they could detect sinister waves of energy wafting through the air. Dupre was so rattled by the tower’s aura that she couldn’t bring herself to touch it. “Babe, it feels ugly,” she said to Smith as she turned away.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯和杜普雷花了一晚时间在圣安东尼奥寻找一座可调查的塔。大多数设施都进不去，外围都是有尖刺铁丝的铁丝网围栏保护。然而这对搭档终于在一个郊区住宅区发现了一座未加防护的基站。当他们走近现场时，被那刺耳而诡异的嗡鸣声吓了一跳。（5G 基站的噪音来自散热风扇和电气部件。）他们相信自己能察觉到某种阴险的能量波在空气中飘荡。杜普雷被塔的氛围震慑得无法碰它。“宝贝，感觉很难受，”她一边转身一边对史密斯说，“它闻起来（看起来）很丑。”

paragraphs.en: Smith pressed forward, however, and laid hands on the tower’s cylindrical metal base. In that moment, he was overwhelmed by sadness at how many people were ignorant of these monstrosities in their midst. “That’s when I really made the decision,” he recalls. “I snapped. Like, man, I’m going to do something about this.”
paragraphs.zh: 然而史密斯还是走了过去，双手摸上了这座塔那圆柱形的金属基座。就在那一刻，他被一种悲伤所淹没：那么多人却对身边这些“怪物”一无所知。“那就是我真正下定决心的时候，”他回忆道。“我崩溃了。就像，伙计，我得对这事做点什么了。”

paragraphs.en: Now that they’d resolved to swing into action, Smith and Dupre discussed how best to battle 5G. They toyed with the idea of launching their own podcast or educational website. But they settled on producing homemade batches of so-called orgonite crystals, which are touted in alternative-medicine circles as having the ability to dampen electromagnetic fields. In the cluttered kitchens of the drug houses where they were hiding, the couple filled muffin tins with tinted resins and snippets of whatever metal they could find—curls of steel wool, coils of copper wire. These would harden into translucent lumps that Smith and Dupre began placing next to San Antonio’s 5G towers, an act of subversion they called “gifting.” Dupre took to creating crystals shaped like hearts or flowers, which she handed out to strangers in the hopes of opening their eyes to the horrors of 5G.
paragraphs.zh: 在他们决定付诸行动后，史密斯和杜普尔便讨论起该如何更有效地对抗5G。他们曾考虑过开设自己的播客或教育网站，但最终决定自己制作所谓的“有机石英晶体”（orgonite）。在替代医学圈子里，这种东西被宣传可以减弱电磁场。两人躲在藏身的毒品窝点里，常常忙着在凌乱的厨房里往小蛋糕烤模里倒入染色树脂，并掺入能找到的各种金属碎片——像是卷成团的钢丝绵、卷线般的铜线。固化后，它们会变成半透明的小块。史密斯和杜普尔开始把这些小块放在圣安东尼奥的5G基站旁边，这种做法他们称为“赠送”。杜普尔则擅长做成心形或花朵造型的晶体，并把它们分给路人，希望借此让人们意识到5G的危害。

paragraphs.en: But Smith quickly soured on gifting as too tepid an approach. He was becoming increasingly radicalized as he learned about individuals who’d made tremendous sacrifices to oppose mobile technology. He particularly admired John Robert Patterson, an Australian telecommunications technician who believed he’d suffered adverse health consequences from overexposure to electromagnetic fields. In 2007, Patterson infamously stole an armored personnel carrier and used it to ram seven mobile towers in Sydney before surrendering to police. The rampage did nothing to slow the growth of Australia’s wireless industry, but Smith liked how Patterson kept preaching about a massive cover-up of mobile technology’s hazards even after leaving prison.
paragraphs.zh: 但史密斯很快就觉得“赠送”太过平淡，不够激进。他在了解了许多为反对移动通信技术付出巨大代价的人后，越来越趋向激进。他尤其佩服澳大利亚电信技术员约翰·罗伯特·帕特森。帕特森坚信自己因电磁场过度接触而受到严重健康损害。2007年，帕特森曾臭名昭著地偷走一辆装甲运兵车，并用它在悉尼撞击了七座移动通信塔，之后向警方自首。那场冲动行为并未减缓澳大利亚无线通讯行业的发展，但史密斯喜欢他的一点：即便出狱后，帕特森仍持续宣扬移动通信技术危险性的巨大掩盖。

paragraphs.en: Smith also paid close attention to the December 25, 2020, bombing in downtown Nashville, during which a loner named Anthony Quinn Warner blew himself up in an RV. The FBI concluded that Warner was motivated by both suicidal desperation and an incoherent set of conspiracy theories, including his belief that the government has been covering up an alien invasion. But Smith focused only on the fact that Warner chose to detonate his vehicle in front of an AT&T network facility—proof, he thought, that 5G was the real intended target.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯还特别关注了2020年12月25日发生在纳什维尔市中心的一起爆炸事件。当时一名名叫安东尼·奎因·华纳的独行者在一辆房车里自爆。联邦调查局认定，华纳既出于自杀式绝望，也受到一套相互矛盾、杂乱无章的阴谋论驱使，其中包括他相信政府在掩盖外星人入侵。但史密斯只抓住一个事实：华纳选择在AT&T网络设施前引爆车辆——在他看来，这证明5G才是这次袭击真正的目标。

paragraphs.en: In February 2021, a massive ice storm knocked out much of the power in San Antonio for days. Smith took advantage of the blackout to trespass at several 5G towers, using bolt cutters to slice through their fences. He studied the fiber-optic cables that extended out of the facilities’ base stations and ran for yards in the open before snaking into the towers’ access hatches. He realized it would be easy to set those exposed cables on fire. He imagined the flames racing up the towers’ innards and consuming the antennas located a hundred feet or more above the ground, transforming them into torches that would mesmerize San Antonians. “I didn’t have any other way to try to get people’s attention,” he says. “I thought maybe, hey, if I burn this over here, maybe someone will see it and be like, ‘Hey, why is someone burning these down?’ And they’d look into it.”
paragraphs.zh: 2021年2月，一场大规模冰暴导致圣安东尼奥大部分地区停电数日。史密斯借此机会翻越几座5G基站围栏，并用切线钳割断铁栅栏。他研究了从基站机房引出的光纤电缆，电缆在基地外裸露一段又一段，随后蜿蜒进入基站的检修口。他意识到，这些外露电缆很容易点着火。于是他想象火焰会沿着塔内结构往上窜，烧到高空百英尺以上的天线，把它们变成能让圣安东尼奥人群惊讶的“火炬”。“我别无他法去吸引大家注意，”他说，“我想，也许就在这儿烧一把火，也许就会有人看到，说——‘喂，为什么有人要把这些都烧掉？’然后他们会开始去查一查。”

paragraphs.en: On the morning of April 10, Smith pulled up to a 5G tower behind a discount eyeglass shop; he’d picked the location so he could make a quick getaway via I-410 across the street. He lit the fuse of a crudely assembled Molotov cocktail and tossed it over the fence. To his dismay, the makeshift bomb landed a bit wide of the cables, causing only minor damage after smashing apart on the ground. Smith fled the scene in such haste that he dropped his lime-green lighter. As he peeled out toward the freeway, he knew he’d have to hone his methods if he was serious about changing the world.
paragraphs.zh: 4月10日清晨，史密斯把车停在一家平价眼镜店后面的一座5G铁塔旁；他之所以选这处，是为了能在对面通过I-410快速离开现场。他点燃了一枚粗制的汽油弹引信并把它掷过铁丝网。令他失望的是，这枚简易爆炸装置偏离了电缆位置，落在地上碎裂后只造成了轻微损伤。史密斯仓促离开时，甚至把他的石灰绿色打火机落在了现场。驶向高速公路的途中，他知道如果真想改变世界，就必须把自己的方法打磨得更狠更准。

paragraphs.en: To become a better arsonist, Smith trekked into a barren corner of the South Texas countryside to run some tests. He tried out grenade-like devices filled with homemade napalm, which he made using a recipe he’d found on the internet. But he ultimately determined that he’d never achieve his desired results by throwing incendiary devices from a fence line—the failure rate would be too high, regardless of the bombs’ design. He instead needed to break into the tower sites so he could burn the cables up close. That would mean spending five minutes or more on the scene, thereby exposing himself to detection and arrest.
paragraphs.zh: 为了成为更厉害的纵火者，史密斯走进了德州南部乡村一处荒凉角落进行试验。他试用了自己在网上找到配方后自制的“果酱弹”式装置，里面装了自制凝固汽油弹（凝固汽油类似火焰推进剂）。但他最终判断，仅靠在栅栏边扔燃烧性装置永远无法达到想要的效果——无论装置设计如何，失手率都太高。于是他改为要闯入铁塔场地，在近距离点燃电缆。那样一来，他得在现场停留五分钟以上，从而更容易被发现并被捕。

paragraphs.en: Smith came up with a simple yet effective modus operandi that would minimize his odds of getting caught. He would scout targets using Google Earth, zeroing in on towers next to wooded areas where he could retreat after setting a fire. He also obtained a wardrobe of costumes he could wear to make it seem like he belonged inside a tower’s perimeter—he stockpiled construction-worker outfits and security-guard uniforms, most of which he acquired in exchange for drugs. As long as he could get away with using his bolt cutters on the fences, no one was likely to question him as he knelt next to the towers and covertly stuffed their cable hatches with accelerant-soaked rags.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯想出了一个简单却很有效的作案方式，以尽量降低被抓住的概率。他会先用谷歌地球勘查目标，锁定靠近林区的铁塔，这样放火后有地方可退。与此同时，他还准备了一套装束，让自己看上去像该区域的正常人员：囤了不少工装和保安制服，其中大多数是靠交换毒品得来的。只要能拿着切线钳在围栏上剪洞溜进去，他在塔旁蹲下并偷偷把浸了助燃剂的破布塞进电缆舱口，就几乎没人会怀疑他。

paragraphs.en: Smith became a rather busy arsonist starting that April: Over an initial six-week period, he hit one tower by a tattoo shop, another outside an upscale apartment complex, and a third at the end of a residential cul-de-sac. Dupre accompanied him on the last of these missions, but she couldn’t bring herself to shimmy through the hole in the fence that Smith had cut. Unlike her boyfriend, she was scared to dirty her hands with actual property destruction.
paragraphs.zh: 从那年四月起，史密斯算是忙碌起来的纵火犯了：在最初六周里，他先后点燃了位于一家纹身店旁的一座铁塔、位于一处高档公寓楼外的一座，以及一座位于住宅死胡同尽头的铁塔。德普随他去完成了最后一次任务，但她做不到和史密斯一样把身体挤进他已经剪开的栅栏洞里。和男友不同，她害怕自己真动手参与财产破坏。

paragraphs.en: After this nerve-racking experience, Dupre pleaded with Smith to halt his arson spree. She did so in part because things were finally looking up for the couple: Thanks to a reference from a prominent San Antonio rapper he knew, Smith had landed an off-the-books job as a handyman at a small commercial building, and the landlord was letting him and Dupre stay in one of the vacant units. Dupre didn’t want Smith to ruin this semi-stable situation by bringing more heat on himself. But Smith was now too deep in the throes of anti-5G fervor. “It gave me meaning,” he says of the fires. “It gave my life more of a meaning, being able to fight against something.”
paragraphs.zh: 经历了这场令人神经紧绷的事件后，达普尔恳求史密斯停止他的纵火狂潮。她之所以这样做，部分原因是这对搭档终于看到了一线希望：凭借她认识的一位圣安东尼奥知名说唱歌手的牵线，史密斯在一栋小型商业楼里找到了一个“脱离正式记录”的维修工活儿，房东还允许他和达普尔住进一间空置单元。达普尔不想让史密斯再给自己找麻烦，打乱这来之不易的半稳定生活。可史密斯当时已经深陷反5G热情的泥潭，难以抽身。“这些火焰给了我意义，”他说，“它让我觉得自己的生活更有意义，能去对抗某种东西。”

paragraphs.en: In late May, Smith set out to torch a tower in the neighborhood of Oak Hills. Perhaps overconfident after having eluded capture for so long, Smith lingered for a while before lighting the gasoline-soaked rags he’d stuffed in the cable hatch. When he finally flicked his lighter, he accidentally ignited the fumes that had built up in the enclosed space. The flames whooshed back into his face, burning off much of his hair and turning his flesh a cherry red.
paragraphs.zh: 在五月下旬，史密斯前往奥克希尔斯地区准备点燃一座塔。也许因为长期躲过抓捕而太过自负，史密斯在点亮被他塞进电缆舱口的浸了汽油的抹布之前犹豫了一会儿。等他最终点亮打火机时，却不小心引燃了封闭空间里堆积的气体。火焰猛地朝他脸上窜回，烧掉了他大半头发，并让他的皮肤变成了樱桃红。

paragraphs.en: Smith stumbled away in agony, knowing that he couldn’t go to an emergency room for help. In a panic he called Dupre, who was working the late shift at a 7-11. She grabbed several jugs of water, locked the store behind her, and sprinted back to the apartment to tend to her boyfriend’s wounds. The couple eventually felt they had no choice but to call Smith’s mother, who put herself in legal jeopardy by coming over to provide care.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯浑身剧痛地跌跌撞撞退开，清楚自己不能去急诊室求助。惊慌之下，他给达普尔打电话。那时达普尔正在7-11上夜班。她抓起几罐水，把店门锁上，飞奔回公寓处理男友的伤势。夫妇俩最后觉得，自己别无选择，只能给史密斯母亲打电话。她进门照料，因而把自己置于法律风险之中。

paragraphs.en: For much of the summer, Smith feared that the physical damage he’d suffered was permanent. Yet even before his face regained its normal complexion and his eyebrows began to resprout, he felt compelled to get back to his arson campaign. Now more cognizant of the risk of self-immolation, he looked for safer ways to start his fires. He found good advice on the website for WarriorUp, a “research project” dedicated to sharing “techniques for sabotaging capitalist infrastructure and extractive industries.” Taking a cue from an article entitled “How to Destroy Cell Phone Towers,” Smith cut up an old tire so he could use the shards in lieu of rags—the rubber caught fire more slowly than cloth. (For the same reason, he also switched to using diesel as his preferred accelerant.)
paragraphs.zh: 整个夏天，史密斯都担心自己遭受的身体伤害是永久性的。然而在他的脸色尚未完全恢复正常、眉毛还没开始重新生长之前，他就感到自己必须回到纵火行动之中。现在他对自焚的风险更有警觉，便寻找更安全的引火方式。他在名为 WarriorUp 的网站上找到了有用建议，那是一个“研究项目”，致力于分享“破坏资本主义基础设施与资源开采行业的技术”。他参考一篇题为《如何摧毁手机信号塔》的文章，剪开了一条旧轮胎，用其中碎片代替抹布来点火——与布料相比，轮胎碎片燃烧得更慢。（基于同样考虑，他还改用柴油作为自己偏好的加速剂。）

paragraphs.en: During one of his attacks, Smith snagged a souvenir that caught his eye: a warning sign that read, “Radio frequency fields near some antennas may exceed the FCC occupational rules for human exposure.” The Federal Communications Commission mandates the posting of these signs for the benefit of maintenance workers who must occasionally climb to the peaks of towers. But Smith mistakenly thought this language amounted to a government confession that civilians within a wide radius of a tower are in constant danger. He delighted in showing off the sign when evangelizing to fellow miscreants about 5G’s role in transforming the US into a repressive dystopia. He could only hope his audiences were listening more intently to his lectures than he’d listened to his prison friend back in 2014.
paragraphs.zh: 在一次行动中，史密斯捡到一个引起他注意的纪念品：一块警示牌，上面写着：“靠近某些天线的无线电频率场可能超过美国联邦通信委员会对人体暴露的职业规范。”联邦通信委员会要求在这些地方张贴标志，以提醒必须定期登上塔顶维护的工作人员。然而史密斯错误地把这段话理解为政府承认，在通信塔较大半径范围内的平民始终处于危险之中。他在向同伙们宣扬 5G 如何把美国变成压迫性反乌托邦时，十分乐于把这块牌子拿出来作为“证据”。他只能希望这些听众比他2014年听过的那位监狱朋友更认真地听他讲课。

paragraphs.en: Jesse Moncada, the lead arson investigator for the San Antonio Fire Department, wasn’t terribly concerned when he inspected the singed 5G tower behind My Econo’s $39.95 Optical on April 10, 2021. Whoever had started the fire that morning was clearly an amateur—their Molotov cocktail hadn’t packed much punch, and they’d foolishly dropped their lime-green lighter at the scene. His best guess was that the culprit was a vagrant bent on causing minor trouble.
paragraphs.zh: 圣安东尼奥消防局的首席纵火调查员杰西·蒙卡达（Jesse Moncada）在2021年4月10日检查My Econo的39.95美元“Optical”店后面的焦黑5G天线时并不担心。当时谁在那个早晨点起了火已经很明显是个新手——他们的燃烧瓶杀伤力不大，而且他们还愚蠢地把石灰绿打火机落在了现场。他最初判断，肇事者大概是个流浪者，只想制造一点小麻烦。

paragraphs.en: “But then it happened again,” says Moncada, who joined the fire department in 2001. “And we started seeing the same pattern and same modes of burning.” Curious as to why someone would be fixated on destroying 5G towers, he contacted the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives for assistance. The agency told him about the cell-tower fires that had occurred in the UK in April 2020, a brief criminal epidemic that had made US federal authorities aware that conspiracy-driven terrorism would inevitably be on the rise. “We assess that violent extremists probably will target a range of telecommunications infrastructure,” the Department of Homeland Security had warned in a May 2020 memo. “More coordinated attacks by multiple individuals in adjacent areas could amplify these incidents.” The document also noted that these extremists were likely to be influenced by a hodgepodge of beliefs: Some would be white nationalists who’d been chattering online about their opposition to Covid restrictions, while others would be radical environmentalists who’d embraced the anti-technology manifesto of the Unabomber, Theodore Kaczynski.
paragraphs.zh: “但后来又发生了一起，”蒙卡达回忆道，他于2001年加入消防队。“我们开始看到同样的痕迹和同样的烧毁方式。”为了弄清为什么有人会对摧毁5G基站如此执着，他联系了酒精、烟草、火器和炸药局（ATF）请求协助。该机构告诉他，2020年4月英国也曾发生过通信塔起火事件，那场短暂的犯罪高发让美国联邦当局意识到，由阴谋论驱动的恐怖主义势必会上升。国土安全部在2020年5月的一份备忘录中警告称：“我们评估，极端暴力分子很可能会袭击多种电信基础设施。”“多个相邻地区多名行动者之间更协调的攻击，可能会放大这类事件。”该文件还指出，这些极端分子很可能受杂糅信仰影响：一些是白人至上主义者，在网上反复传播他们反对新冠限制的言论；另一些则是激进环保主义者，已经接受了“联邦快递炸弹客”西奥多·卡齐辛斯基（Theodore Kaczynski）的反技术宣言。

paragraphs.en: Now aware that he was likely dealing with a form of terrorism rooted in online disinformation, Moncada had fingerprints lifted off the lighter he’d found at the first crime scene. They matched those of Smith, who had been wanted on a fugitive felony warrant since the previous July. At another burnt tower, Moncada also found a black glove that contained flecks of Smith’s DNA. But it was one thing to know the arsonist’s identity, quite another to find him: Smith was a ghost drifting through San Antonio’s underworld, leaving few if any traces as he cycled through an endless stream of prepaid phones.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，蒙卡达已认识到自己很可能面对的是一种根植于网络错误信息的恐怖主义形态，于是他从第一次现场捡到的打火机上提取了指纹。指纹与史密斯一致。史密斯自上一年7月起就以逃犯身份悬赏通缉、涉嫌重罪。又在另一座被烧毁的塔下，蒙卡达找到了一只黑色手套，里面含有史密斯的DNA碎片。但知道纵火者身份是一回事，找到他则是另一回事：史密斯像个幽灵般在圣安东尼奥地下世界里飘忽不定，在一串又一串预付费手机之间来回切换，几乎不留任何痕迹。

paragraphs.en: Hoping to glean a license plate number or accomplice’s description that might help reveal Smith’s location, Moncada contacted the security teams at affected companies such as Verizon and T-Mobile. “But what made it difficult was they didn’t want anybody to know that their towers were being damaged,” he says. “I didn’t get enough help from them. So it was difficult for me to put everything together, because I didn’t have any video footage or witnesses.”
paragraphs.zh: 为了获得可能揭示史密斯位置的车牌号或帮凶特征描述，蒙卡达联系了受损企业的安保团队，例如 Verizon 和 T-Mobile。“但困难在于，他们不想让任何人知道自己的基站被破坏，”他解释道。“他们没有给我足够的帮助。所以我很难把线索拼起来，因为我没有任何视频监控录像，也没有目击者。”

paragraphs.en: By the spring of 2022, the number of 5G arsons was nearing 20, and Moncada began collaborating with both the Texas Rangers and the FBI to solve the case. These larger agencies helped process more DNA evidence—at a tower that burned in March 2022, for example, Moncada recovered pubic hairs from a pair of boxer shorts that Smith used to start the fire. But there seemed little chance of stopping the attacks unless Smith made a mistake.
paragraphs.zh: 到2022年春季，5G 基站纵火案的数量已经接近20起，蒙卡达开始与德州游骑兵队和联邦调查局合作破解此案。这些更大的机构帮助处理更多 DNA 证据——例如在2022年3月被烧毁的一座基站上，蒙卡达从史密斯用来点燃火焰的一条平角内裤里发现了阴毛。但除非史密斯犯错，这些攻击似乎都难以停止。

paragraphs.en: His ego inflated by his months of success, Smith took to thinking of himself as an “urban gray man,” capable of committing his arsons with supreme speed and stealth. He would often persuade an unwitting acquaintance to drive him to within a block or two of the tower he’d picked to torch. He’d hop out of the car and say he had to make a quick drug sale, then change into the reflective vest and hard hat that he kept in his backpack. He’d snip the fence, start the fire, and slip into the woods as the flames began to swell. He’d then strip off his construction-worker gear, pull a baseball cap low over his eyes, and circle back to the car as if nothing unusual had happened. As they pulled away, he’d peer out the window at the clouds of smoke now rising above the city.
paragraphs.zh: 在连续几个月成功作案之后，他的自负不断膨胀，开始把自己当成“城市灰衣人”，以为自己能以最快、最隐秘的方式实施纵火。为了到达他选定的目标基站，他常常说服一个毫不知情的熟人开车送他到距离目标一两条街的地方。他下车后会说自己得去完成一笔快速的毒品交易，然后换上背包里随身带着的反光背心和安全帽。接着他会剪断围栏、点燃火源，趁火势蔓延进入树林。待火势起势后，他就脱掉工地工装，低低扣起棒球帽，像什么都没发生过一样绕回来上车。离开时，他会透过窗户看着如今正升起于城市上空的烟柱。

paragraphs.en: Smith’s personal life was growing messy as he became ever more focused on leading the revolution against 5G. He lost his handyman job in early 2022, a development that forced him and Dupre to relocate to a friend’s apartment. The couple’s romance was also beginning to falter, in large part because Dupre had tired of centering her life around the use of Xanax and methamphetamine. “I was miserable,” she says. “I hated every second of it.” Smith, meanwhile, started seeing another woman named Callie Holland, who had recently moved down from Missouri with her daughter. On one of their dates, she watched him burn a 5G tower.
paragraphs.zh: 随着他越来越专注于领导“反对5G”的革命，史密斯的个人生活也越发混乱。2022年初他失去了杂工工作，导致他和杜普雷不得不搬去住在一个朋友的公寓。该对的感情也开始动摇，主要原因之一是杜普雷已经厌倦了让自己的生活围绕着阿普唑仑（Xanax）和甲基苯丙胺来过。她说：“我很痛苦，整个人都很痛苦。我讨厌其中的每一秒。”与此同时，史密斯开始与另一名名叫凯莉·霍兰德的女子交往，她刚刚带着女儿从密苏里搬了下来。在一次约会中，她目睹了他烧掉一座5G基站。

paragraphs.en: As he passed the one-year anniversary of his first arson, Smith was feeling nothing short of invincible. On April 29, 2022, he chose to deviate from his usual cautious approach and set fire to a tower next to a heavily trafficked Walmart Supercenter. When Moncada arrived on the scene to investigate, he reviewed the store’s security footage and spotted a red 2017 Chevrolet Cruze zooming out of the parking lot moments after the fire’s ignition. The car was registered to Holland, who had previously been arrested, though not charged, for a drug violation—precisely the sort of person who might run in the same sordid circles as Smith.
paragraphs.zh: 在他度过首次纵火行动一周年时，史密斯几乎可以说是感觉自己势不可挡。2022 年 4 月 29 日，他故意偏离了一贯的谨慎做法，在一家人流密集的沃尔玛超级中心旁边点燃了一座电信塔。蒙卡达到现场调查后查看了商店的监控录像，并注意到一辆红色的 2017 款雪佛兰克鲁兹在起火几分钟后从停车场迅速驶出。该车登记在霍兰德名下，她先前因毒品违法行为被捕过，但未被起诉；这类人物正是可能与史密斯混在同一肮脏圈子里的人。

paragraphs.en: In the wee hours of May 13, the San Antonio police detained Holland as she left a Mexican restaurant. She admitted that she’d loaned her car to Smith two weeks earlier, and she spilled all she knew about her part-time lover’s crusade. “Holland advised that Smith stated the US government is out to get him and that the 5G towers give off radiation and control minds,” a Texas Ranger wrote in his report. “Holland stated that Smith watches a bunch of videos of 5G towers on YouTube.”
paragraphs.zh: 5 月 13 日凌晨，圣安东尼奥警方在她离开一家墨西哥餐馆时拘留了霍兰德。她承认自己两周前曾把车借给史密斯，并把自己对这位“兼职情人”所坚持的理念运动所知悉的情况全部说了出来。德州游骑兵队一名警员在报告中写道：“霍兰德表示，史密斯称美国政府想要针对他，而且 5G 塔会发射辐射并控制人脑。”报告还写道：“霍兰德称史密斯会在 YouTube 上看大量关于 5G 塔的视频。”

paragraphs.en: Most importantly, Holland provided the investigators with a phone number for Smith that was only a week old—a critical piece of information that Moncada had been seeking for months. By noon that day, Moncada had obtained a warrant to “ping” Smith’s phone—that is, to triangulate its location using the same towers that Smith reviled as tools of oppression.
paragraphs.zh: 最关键的是，霍兰德提供了一个仅有一周历史的史密斯电话号码给调查人员，这是蒙卡达数月来一直在寻找的关键线索。当天中午，蒙卡达就拿到了对该号码进行“定向定位”监测的授权许可——也就是利用史密斯口中那些被他称为压迫工具的同样基站来三角测定其位置。

paragraphs.en: A US Secret Service agent performed the ping, which indicated that Smith was at an apartment complex behind a Lowe’s home improvement store. A phalanx of police officers, Texas Rangers, and FBI agents immediately descended on the area in search of the arsonist who had set fire to 22 5G towers since April 2021.
paragraphs.zh: 一名美国特勤局特工执行了这次定位，结果显示史密斯当时在一家劳氏家居改造连锁店后面的公寓区。警员、德州游骑兵队和联邦调查局特工随即成批赶往现场，寻找这名自 2021 年 4 月以来共纵火了 22 座 5G 塔的纵火嫌疑人。

paragraphs.en: At 1:20 pm that afternoon, Smith emerged from his apartment and hopped into a friend’s Cadillac CTS. As they rolled away from the curb, the car was surrounded by dozens of law enforcement agents with handguns and rifles drawn.
paragraphs.zh: 那天下午一点二十分，史密斯从公寓走出来，跳上了一位朋友的凯迪拉克 CTS。当他们刚从路边离开时，汽车周围已被数十名持枪执法人员包围，这些人手里都握着手枪和步枪。

paragraphs.en: Smith had always known this day would come, and he’d often contemplated how he would react. He liked the idea of going down in a blaze of glory, of martyring himself for the anti-5G cause—there was romance in the concept of becoming a more extreme figure in technophobic lore than the Australian who’d bulldozed the Sydney towers. Given that he was armed with a loaded handgun, that outcome was certainly an option. But in the face of such an awesome amount of firepower pointed right at him, Smith froze.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯早就知道这一天会到来，也常常想象自己会如何应对。他很喜欢“在荣耀中陨落”的想法，想为反5G事业以身殉道——他觉得成为反科技恐惧叙事里比那位用推土机拆了悉尼基站的澳大利亚人更极端的人物，有一种浪漫色彩。既然他手里有把装了弹的手枪，这种结局当然是个可行选择。但面对直指自己、令人窒息的火力优势，史密斯还是僵住了。

paragraphs.en: “This is the happiest I’ve ever been.”
paragraphs.zh: “这是我这辈子最开心的时候。”

paragraphs.en: Smith could tell I looked quizzical when I heard him utter those words, and he quickly qualified his statement by adding, “I know that sounds crazy.” The place where we were meeting, the visiting room of a desolate south Texas prison, was entirely devoid of cheer. But Smith, whose forehead still bears scars from a long-ago pistol whipping, explained that despite his grim surroundings, he has never felt better. Drug-free for the first time in ages, he now spends his days studying college-level chemistry, reading the philosophy of Jean-Jacques Rousseau, and listening to political podcasts like Pod Save America. He counts himself blessed that he didn’t choose to commit suicide-by-cop. “I needed a wake-up call,” he says. “I needed to get away from everything and rethink everything and have a chance to sit down and reevaluate.”
paragraphs.zh: 当我听到他这么说时，能看出我一脸疑惑，于是史密斯很快补了一句：“我知道这听起来疯了。” 我们见面的地方——一间偏僻的德州南部监狱会见室，完全没有半点欢乐气氛。但史密斯表示，尽管环境凄凉，他从未像现在这样好过。多年来首次摆脱毒品后，他现在整天学习大学水平的化学课程，阅读让-雅克·卢梭的哲学，还听《Pod Save America》这类政治播客。回想起自己没有选择“被警察枪杀式自杀”，他自认是被幸运眷顾了。“我需要一个惊醒自己的闹铃，”他说，“我需要离开一切、重新思考一切，有机会坐下来重新评估。”

paragraphs.en: He also considers himself fortunate to have a shot at leaving prison before his 40th birthday. After he pleaded guilty to six counts of arson in federal court, prosecutors were keen to turn him into an example and sought a 15-year sentence—far longer than recommended by the advisory guidelines. “The sentencing guidelines do not contemplate a 22-tower arson spree meant to shut down the cellphone system to follow a bizarre anti-government philosophy,” wrote the lead prosecutor, who specializes in counterterrorism cases. “The increased focus and attacks on this critical infrastructure by extremists and conspiracy theorists like the Defendant has the potential to wreak extensive societal damage and disruption.”
paragraphs.zh: 他也认为自己很幸运，能在40岁生日之前获得离开监狱的机会。因在联邦法庭对六项纵火罪认罪后，检方本想树立典型，要求判处15年刑期，这远高于建议量刑指南的区间。主审检察官写道：“该量刑指南并未预见到为实施一种怪异的反政府理念而对22座基站实施纵火狂潮，目的在于瘫痪蜂窝通信系统。”这位检察官专门负责反恐案件。“像被告这样极端分子和阴谋论者对这类关键基础设施持续加大关注并发动攻击，可能造成广泛的社会性损害和扰乱。”

paragraphs.en: Frightened by the prospect of spending a decade and a half in prison, Smith sent handwritten requests for help to several organizations that he thought might be sympathetic to his plight. The recipients included Children’s Health Defense, a nonprofit founded by Robert F. Kennedy Jr.; Smith knew that at an anti-vaccine rally in early 2022, Kennedy had declared that 5G was designed to “harvest our data and control our behavior.” No one ever replied to Smith’s entreaties, but the judge still cut him a break: He was sentenced to 78 months of federal time, to be served concurrently with a state sentence that’s currently projected to run until 2030. (When he was arrested in May 2022, the fugitive Smith was carrying a half-pound of cannabis in addition to his handgun; he will be eligible for parole on the state charges next year.)
paragraphs.zh: 担心自己要在监狱里度过十多年之后，斯密斯给几家他认为可能同情自己处境的机构写了手写求助信。收信机构包括罗伯特·弗兰克·肯尼迪二世创立的儿童健康防御组织（Children’s Health Defense）；斯密斯记得在2022年初的一次反疫苗集会上，肯尼迪曾宣布，5G是为了“收集我们的数据并控制我们的行为”而设计的。没有任何人回应他的恳求，但法官仍给了他一个机会：他被判处78个月联邦刑期，并与目前预计将执行到2030年的州级刑期并行执行。（他在2022年5月被逮捕时，这名在逃的斯密斯除了手枪外，还携带了半磅大麻；他将在明年有资格就州指控申请假释。）

paragraphs.en: Dupre was arrested for arson, too, after admitting to Moncada that she’d been with Smith when he torched a tower in May 2021. That case was eventually dismissed, however, and Dupre has transformed her life in the time since. Now clean and sober, she trained to become a substance-abuse counselor at San Antonio College and recently took a job at a recovery center. “I need to correct my karma and help other addicts,” she told me.
paragraphs.zh: 杜普尔也因纵火被捕，因为她承认在2021年5月与斯密斯一同在她点燃一座基站时在场。然而该案最终被撤销，杜普尔也在此后重塑了自己的人生。如今她戒毒并戒酒，现在在圣安东尼奥学院受训成为一名物质滥用顾问，最近在一家戒毒康复中心找到工作。“我需要弥补我的因果业报，并帮助其他成瘾者，”她对我说。

paragraphs.en: She has extricated herself from the darkness of her former life, but Dupre remains convinced that 5G poses an existential threat. She is far from unique in holding onto that belief. This past June, for example, arsonists set fire to six cell towers in Belfast, Northern Ireland. Other Covid-related conspiracy theories have endured and mutated, too. In June, a Minnesota man was charged with assassinating a state lawmaker and her husband, later saying he had meant to punish those who supported Covid vaccines; later in the summer, an Atlanta man who believed he’d been harmed by one of those vaccines opened fire on the headquarters of the Centers for Disease Control, killing a police officer.
paragraphs.zh: 她已经摆脱了过去黑暗生活的纠缠，但杜普尔仍然坚信5G构成生死存亡的威胁。她在这一信念上的执着并不罕见。就在今年6月，纵火者在北爱尔兰贝尔法斯特纵火烧毁了六座基站。其他与新冠相关的阴谋论同样仍在延续并不断变形。6月，一名明尼苏达男子被控谋杀一名州议员及其丈夫，后来他称自己本想惩罚支持新冠疫苗的人；同年夏天，另一名居住在亚特兰大的男子因相信自己受到某种疫苗伤害，于是对美国疾病控制与预防中心（CDC）总部开枪，击毙一名警察。

paragraphs.en: “I don’t know how to say this without sounding crazy, but I just don’t trust the technology,” Dupre says of 5G. “I don’t think that they’re just cell phone towers.” She also has sympathy for the choices made by Smith, with whom she maintains a close platonic friendship: “I support him 100 percent. I’m not saying what he did was right, but I’m not saying it was wrong.”
paragraphs.zh: “我不想说得太离谱，但我就是不信任这项技术，”Dupre 谈到 5G 时说。“我不认为它们只是手机信号塔。” 她对 Smith 的选择也表示理解，毕竟她与他是关系密切的柏拉图式朋友：“我百分之百支持他。我并不是说他做的是对的，但我也不说他做的是错的。”

paragraphs.en: Smith maintains his vehement opposition to 5G, but the more pressing object of his concern these days is AI—the newest fixation among young extremists now sprouting from the same media ecosystem that radicalized Smith. “It’s clear to them that AI will be utilized in order for the government to maintain its control, to enhance its ability to surveil, to monitor, to track, to interfere with our privacy,” says Arie Perliger, a professor at the University of Massachusetts Lowell who studies domestic terrorism. Crude individual attacks against AI data centers seem inevitable as the technology increasingly upends the routines of American life. The question is whether such violence will elicit more public sympathy than Smith’s fires, even if perpetrated by militants who espouse outlandish political beliefs; the grain of truth in AI paranoia is many orders of magnitude larger than the one at the core of the anti-5G movement.
paragraphs.zh: Smith 仍然坚持自己强烈反对 5G，但他如今更紧迫的担忧对象是 AI——这种新型执念在年轻极端分子中正越来越盛行，而这类人也都出自同一套媒体生态，正是这一生态曾使 Smith 走向激进化。马萨诸塞州洛厄尔大学（UMass Lowell）研究国内恐怖主义的教授 Arie Perliger 说：“对他们来说，很清楚 AI 会被用来帮助政府维持控制，增强其监控能力、监视能力、追踪能力，并干涉我们的隐私。” 对 AI 数据中心的粗暴个人化袭击似乎不可避免，因为这项技术正日益颠覆美国人的日常生活。问题在于，这种暴力是否会比 Smith 的纵火事件赢得更多公众同情，即便实施者是持有离谱政治信念的激进分子；AI 偏执中“真实部分”的分量，比反 5G 运动核心里的那一点真相要大得多。

paragraphs.en: “If I had one message I could communicate about my beliefs, it would be, ‘Why would the most creative and imaginative beings on Earth wish to create something that would make us obsolete?’” Smith told me. Once an enthusiastic supporter of President Trump—he says he nearly went to Washington, DC, on January 6, 2021—he now worries the current administration is glad to let giant AI companies make humans subordinate to machines.
paragraphs.zh: “如果我只想传达一条关于自己信念的信息，那就是：‘地球上最有创造力、最具想象力的生命，为什么要去创造一个会让我们变得过时的东西？’” Smith 对我说。

paragraphs.en: Smith dreams of launching a podcast about the dangers of technology, though he acknowledges that people will be reluctant to listen to someone with his unsavory background. He insisted to me that he deeply regrets setting his fires, because doing so harmed his ability to become a trusted political voice. Yet toward the end of our time together in Texas, Smith asked if I wanted to see something cool. After making sure no guards were watching, he rose from his seat and smiled as he hiked up the left pant-leg of his white prison jumpsuit. Splashed across his entire calf was a beautifully rendered tattoo that he’d recently had inked by a fellow inmate. It shows a 5G tower engulfed in flames.
paragraphs.zh: Smith 梦想开设一个关于技术风险的播客，尽管他承认人们可能不愿意听一个背后有着不光彩经历的人。“我非常后悔自己点燃了那些火”，他对我坚持说，因为这让他失去了成为可信政治声音的机会。但在我们最后一次在德州见面时，Smith 问我是否想看点“很酷的东西”。确认没有保安在看着后，他从椅子上站起，微笑着把白色监狱囚服左腿抬高。整条小腿上，铺展开一枚他刚让同牢犯人纹好的精美纹身，正是一座被大火吞没的 5G 塔。
